name: PersonioConnector
description: Connects to the Personio HR platform (Employees, Attendances, Time-Offs, Documents, Custom Attributes, Departments, Offices, Projects, Recruitment Applications + Applicants). Uses OAuth2 client-credentials authentication with rotating bearer tokens — the bearer changes on every response, so the connector captures the Authorization response header and uses it on the next request.

install_fields.label: Personio Client ID
install_fields.placeholder: Enter your Personio API Client ID
install_fields.help: Generate at Personio → Settings → API Credentials. Shown alongside the secret at creation time.

install_fields.label: Personio Client Secret
install_fields.placeholder: Enter your Personio API Client Secret
install_fields.help: Paired secret for the Client ID. Shown once at creation — store it securely. The connector encrypts it at rest.

install_fields.label: Partner ID
install_fields.help: X-Personio-Partner-ID header value. Leave blank to use the Shielva default.

install_fields.label: App ID
install_fields.help: X-Personio-App-ID header value. Leave blank to use the Shielva default.

install_fields.label: Personio API Base URL
install_fields.help: Base URL for the Personio REST API. Leave blank to use the default.

install_fields.label: Rate Limit (requests/min)
install_fields.help: Maximum Personio API requests per minute. Default is 60.

apis.name: Install Connector
apis.description: Validates client_id and client_secret and persists install-time config. Does not call Personio.

apis.name: Authenticate
apis.description: POST /auth — exchanges client_id + client_secret for a rotating bearer token. The token is captured from the Authorization response header.

apis.name: Health Check
apis.description: Verifies Personio API connectivity by listing one employee (GET /company/employees?limit=1).

apis.name: List Employees
apis.description: GET /company/employees — paginated employee list with optional email / updated_since filters.

apis.name: Get Employee
apis.description: GET /company/employees/{id} — fetch a single employee by Personio numeric ID.

apis.name: Create Employee
apis.description: POST /company/employees — create a new employee record.

apis.name: Update Employee
apis.description: PATCH /company/employees/{id} — patch one or more attributes on an existing employee.

apis.name: List Attendances
apis.description: GET /company/attendances — list attendance entries for the given employees and date range.

apis.name: Create Attendance
apis.description: POST /company/attendances — record a single attendance entry for an employee.

apis.name: Update Attendance
apis.description: PATCH /company/attendances/{id} — patch attributes on an attendance entry.

apis.name: List Time-Offs
apis.description: GET /company/time-offs — list time-off (absence) entries, optionally filtered by date range.

apis.name: Create Time-Off
apis.description: POST /company/time-offs — book a time-off entry for an employee.

apis.name: List Time-Off Types
apis.description: GET /company/time-off-types — list the time-off types configured in the account.

apis.name: List Documents
apis.description: GET /company/document-categories — list document categories visible to the given employee.

apis.name: Upload Document
apis.description: POST /company/employees/{id}/documents — multipart upload of a document into the employee file.

apis.name: List Custom Attributes
apis.description: GET /company/employees/custom-attributes — list the custom employee attributes configured in Personio.

apis.name: List Departments
apis.description: GET /company/departments — list the departments configured in Personio.

apis.name: List Offices
apis.description: GET /company/offices — list the offices configured in Personio.

apis.name: List Projects
apis.description: GET /company/projects — list the projects configured in Personio.

apis.name: List Recruitment Applications
apis.description: GET /recruiting/applications — list ATS applications, optionally filtered by status.

apis.name: Get Applicant
apis.description: GET /recruiting/applicants/{id} — fetch an applicant profile.
